capacity.basis: capacity-bound: 2 machines × 20 hr/day parallel pool; production = ceil(50·4.514hr ÷ (2×20)) = 6 d

routing.reasoning: Axisymmetric cross-section (round, turnable): axis 60mm × Ø39mm → CNC turning / mill-turn. A round metal part is rarely powder-bed printed at production volume.

notes: DFM note: processes cnc_3axis, forging, investment_casting are costed but flagged NOT DFM-ready as-modeled (the engine reports ERROR-level blockers — these parts were modeled for 3D printing, so molding/casting lack draft). Their cost shows the tooling economics *if* the part is redesigned for that process. Set strict_dfm=True to exclude them entirely.

Geometric routing: this part reads as a 'rotational' → cnc_turning. Axisymmetric cross-section (round, turnable): axis 60mm × Ø39mm → CNC turning / mill-turn. A round metal part is rarely powder-bed printed at production volume.
Absolute cost is ±40–60% (cycle-time/tooling defaults). The crossover quantity and make-vs-buy direction are robust to it because they depend on the fixed-vs-variable split, driven by your rates.

assumptions.name: stock_allowance
assumptions.source: CNC billet oversize (milling: on bounding box; turning: on hull)

assumptions.name: machine_labor_frac
assumptions.source: operator-labor share of the machine rate that scales with region (capital/facility stays global) [assumption, not shop-validated]

assumptions.name: perishable_frac
assumptions.source: perishable tooling/consumables as a fraction of CNC machine cost [assumption, not shop-validated]

decision.note: Make by cnc_turning (Ti6Al4V (Wrought)) — $112.69/unit at qty 1, the cheapest make-as-is option and your low-volume pick. cnc_turning is cheapest at every quantity tested — no tooling crossover.

identity.reason: no org corpus yet — falls back to standard-catalog + file metadata (never a fabricated identity)
caveats: a retrieved SUGGESTION to confirm, not a verified identity; retrieval can be wrong — check before trusting.
geometry_similarity is a documented proxy (exp(-z_scored_L2 / 2.0)), monotonic in geometric closeness — NOT a probability.
identity.provenance: RETRIEVED (org corpus: geometry k-NN + name match)
